history.title: ‘CPUs are cool again,' Intel and AMD reporting spikes in CPU demand due to agentic AI, shortages — Lisa Su says business exceeded expectations while Intel is looking at long-term agreements with potential customers
history.description: Both AMD and Intel reports increased demand for CPUs, driven by AI agents' need for general computing power.
history.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 14 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Tom's Hardware Get the Tom's Hardware Newsletter Get Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox. Contact me with news and offers from other Future brands Receive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors By submitting your information you agree to the Terms & Conditions and Privacy Policy and are aged 16 or over. You are now subscribed Your newsletter sign-up was successful An account already exists for this email address, please log in. Subscribe to our newsletter Both AMD and Intel said during the 2026 Morgan Stanley Technology, Media & Telecom Conference that demand for CPUs is seeing an uptick due to artificial intelligence. Intel CFO David Zinsner said during his question and answer (via Investing.com) that “the CPU has become cool again this year,” especially as AI agents need CPUs to orchestrate the computationally-heavy tasks that the GPUs and NPUs will execute. It has even started seeing customers who are looking at long-term agreements, ensuring that they’ll have a continuous supply of these chips needed to expand their operations. On the other hand, AMD CEO Lisa Su said during the same conference, “You know, we’re seeing a significant CPU demand, frankly, as a result of the inference demand picking up.” She also added later that “the CPU portion of the business has actually far exceeded my expectations in terms of demand.”Go deeper with TH Premium: CPU (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)CPU scaling with DLSSRyzen to the top: How AMD innovated in the gaming CPU marketHow ARM is working its way into PCsAMD CES 2026 gaming trends press Q&A roundtable transcriptThe AI boom has been driving a shortage in various components since ChatGPT showed some potential in late 2022. It first began with GPUs, especially as data centers and hyperscalers have been buying these components en masse to build massive servers with hundreds of thousands of GPUs. As graphics card supply started to normalize around the middle of 2025, experts and analysts have started warning about memory and storage chip shortages due to massive demand for high-bandwidth memory and enterprise-grade storage in AI data centers.We felt the full swing of this crisis in the fourth quarter of last year, with pricing for RAM modules and SSDs continuing to rise through February 2026. What makes this worse than the GPU shortage is that it has a much wider impact. While the GPUs that were in short supply were mostly limited to desktop PCs and gaming laptops with a discrete graphics card, virtually every modern digital device — from consumer devices like smart TVs and smartphones to automobiles and industrial-grade equipment — needs memory and storage. And consumer-grade memory and storage is fighting for the same wafer space that enterprise-grade memory and storage could occupy, usually with a far higher price tag. You may like Intel, AMD server CPUs reportedly suffering from supply shortages in China AMD Zen 6 and Intel Nova Lake CPUs reportedly arriving late, delayed to CES 2027 'We can't completely vacate the client market' says Intel amid wafer supply shortages As AI advancements move forward from large language models and chatbots to agents that can observe, reason, plan, act, and learn independently, data centers require more multi-processor computing power — that means combining CPUs, GPUs, NPUs, and more — to support the entire agentic AI workflow. China is starting to see this spike in demand, with both Team Blue and Team Red reporting supply shortages for server CPUs in the region. We’re also seeing a spike in demand for high-end Mac Studios and Mac minis, especially as we see more people build their own local AI agents with the rise in popularity of the open-source Clawdbot/Moltbot/OpenClaw.AMD and Intel are presumably talking about data center demand for their CPUs; consumer systems aren't equipped to handle the massive memory demands of agentic AI. If there is a shortage, however, it could trickle down to the consumer market, assuming the demand keeps pace.Over the past several generations, AMD and Intel have converged their data center and consumer offerings, allowing them to maximize yields by leveraging the same microarchitecture across both client and enterprise. Some of that silicon won't be useful in the data center, so the consumer market won't evaporate. But it could put downward pressure on supply if the focus shifts toward the data center, as we've seen with RAM and SSDs.Unlike Nvidia, which has seen exponential increases in revenue in its data center business, both AMD and Intel still see about half of their total revenue each quarter from the consumer market. It's still an important market, so although demand from data centers may increase, it shouldn't come at the cost of the consumer market, at least entirely.Hopefully, both Intel and AMD can keep up with the future demand, so as not to exacerbate the worsening situation of the computer industry. Otherwise, some are already predicting the end of the entry-level PC by 2028 if things continue as they are.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors Follow Tom's Hardware on Google News, or add us as a preferred source, to get our latest news, analysis, & reviews in your feeds.
history.author: Jowi Morales
history.titleZh: CPU因智能体AI需求激增再度走俏，供应短缺下英特尔与AMD财报飘红——苏姿丰称业绩超预期，英特尔拟与潜在客户签署长期协议
history.descriptionZh:
在2026年摩根士丹利技术、媒体与电信大会上，AMD和英特尔高层均指出，人工智能的快速发展正在显著提振中央处理器（CPU）的市场需求。英特尔首席财务官戴维·津斯纳表示，“CPU今年再次变得炙手可热”，他强调，AI智能体需要CPU来协调和调度GPU及NPU执行那些计算密集型任务，这重新确立了CPU的核心地位。甚至已有客户寻求签订长期协议，以确保获得持续稳定的芯片供应以支持其业务扩张。AMD首席执行官苏姿丰同样指出，由于AI推理需求增长，公司正经历“显著的CPU需求”，并且“CPU业务部分的实际需求远超预期”。

这一趋势的背景是，自2022年底ChatGPT展现出潜力以来，AI热潮已引发多轮零部件短缺。短缺最初集中在GPU领域，数据中心和超大规模供应商大规模采购以构建搭载数十万GPU的庞大服务器集群。随着2025年中旬显卡供应逐步恢复正常，行业专家又开始预警，由于AI数据中心对高带宽内存和企业级存储的巨量需求，内存和存储芯片可能面临短缺。这场危机在2025年第四季度全面显现，DRAM模块和SSD的价格持续上涨至2026年2月。与相对局限于台式机和游戏笔记本的GPU短缺不同，内存和存储短缺的影响范围更广，因为从智能电视、智能手机到汽车和工业设备，几乎所有现代数字设备都需要这些组件，而消费级产品正在与利润通常更高的企业级产品争夺有限的晶圆产能。

技术层面上，AI正从大型语言模型和聊天机器人，向能够观察、推理、规划、行动和自主学习的“智能体”演进。这要求数据中心提供更强大的多处理器计算能力——即协同使用CPU、GPU、NPU等——以支持完整的智能体AI工作流程。CPU在此架构中的关键作用在于“编排”，负责管理复杂任务流程和资源分配，而GPU和NPU则专注于并行计算和神经网络推理加速。这种架构演进是推动数据中心CPU需求激增的核心技术动因。

市场影响已经开始显现。在中国市场，英特尔和AMD均已报告服务器CPU出现供应短缺。同时，由于开源项目如Clawdbot/Moltbot/OpenClaw的流行，促使更多人构建本地AI智能体，高端Mac Studio和Mac mini的需求也出现激增。

尽管AMD和英特尔谈论的需求增长主要针对数据中心，但潜在的供应紧张可能蔓延至消费市场。过去几代产品中，两家公司通过在同代微架构上衍生出数据中心和消费级产品，以最大化晶圆利用率和良率。部分不适合数据中心的晶圆会转向消费市场，因此消费级产品不会完全消失。然而，如果产能重心向利润率更高的数据中心倾斜，消费市场的供应将面临压力，正如我们在内存和SSD市场所见。与数据中心业务收入呈指数级增长的英伟达不同，AMD和英特尔每季度仍有约一半的收入来自消费市场。这是一个重要市场，因此数据中心需求的增长不应完全以牺牲消费市场为代价。

如果英特尔和AMD无法满足未来需求，可能会加剧计算机行业的紧张局势。甚至有预测指出，若情况持续，到2028年入门级PC市场可能面临消亡。行业希望两家巨头能平衡好供需，以支撑整个生态的健康发展。

history.title: Go beyond the review with Bench, the deepest consumer hardware benchmarking database on the internet — compare hundreds of products across a range of categories
history.description: Take advantage of Bench, the ultimate resource for PC hardware enthusiasts, offering a large database of benchmarks for you to browse through. We detail how the tool works, and what we're doing to keep results up-to-date here.
history.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 0 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Tom's Hardware Get the Tom's Hardware Newsletter Get Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox. Subscribe to our newsletter In 2025, we introduced Tom's Hardware Premium, an even deeper dive into the world of tech and DIY computing, which you can access through our website for a small fee. While that includes exclusive news analysis, features and hardware roadmaps, the Premium offering also sports a large database of benchmark test results, named Bench.Bench is a performance analysis tool that gives you deeper access into how a given product performs in real-world and synthetic benchmarks. Using it, you can compare two different products in a given category, offering you the ability to view benchmark data between products with a simple click.Bench is a Tom's Hardware Premium exclusive feature, and you can get access to it — as well as everything THP has to offer — by subscribing now for as little as $3 a month. You may like Get Tom's Hardware Premium for less than $3 a month — access our hardware roadmaps, deeper analysis, exclusive features, and unlock the Bench database Behind the scenes of our massive CPU retest for Bench — testing at 1080p, choosing new apps, and gathering data for a decade of CPUs Here are the tools I use as Tom’s Hardware resident CPU reviewer — a cheap aluminum open bench, thermal paste wipes, platform-labeled external power buttons, and more Tom's Hardware Premium Subcription: $29 at tomshardware.comDon’t miss out on Tom’s Hardware Premium. Get a full year of access for just $29: daily news analysis, deep dives into specialist topics in the semiconductor industry, as well as access to Bench, the largest benchmarking database around.View DealWith that out of the way, here's a sneak preview at what you can expect when subscribed. Let's take a look at a CPU comparison table to kick things off. Let's pit the AMD Ryzen 9 9950X3D against Intel's Core Ultra 9 285K.First, all I have to do is hit the compare tab and select the category, which spans CPUs, GPUs, Laptops, Monitors, and SSDs. Once we're in the CPU category, we can select the two products. Then you'll see a comparison between all of the data points we recorded for those two CPUs in alphabetical order.Image 1 of 3First, we select the category.(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)Once the products are selected, you can scroll through benchmarks.(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)There is a wealth of data to browse through.(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)As well as the comparison tool, Bench offers tables of data per category. So, if you're looking to see which part performs the best in a certain workload, you can do that too.For the next example, we're going to pick out a specific GPU workload and see which consumer graphics card performs best in that scenario. Let's take the Sony hit God of War: Ragnarok. If you are building a mid-range system, then 1440p or QHD is a good spot to select for this benchmark. Since we're focused on the mid-range, let's take a look at the middle of the table. Here, we can see that the Radeon 9060 XT is faster than Nvidia's RTX 5060. These two GPUs hit around the same price point, so if I were a God of War fanatic looking to save, that would be my pick.Image 1 of 2(Image credit: Toms Hardware)(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)How we run BenchBench offers tons of data to browse through; these are just two examples of how you can use the tool to browse through hardware benchmarks. While the database is primarily built from the work completed while reviewing a given product, the data that you see in reviews is often truncated into geomean values, or put into a certain workload "bucket."Of course, while Bench has been live since last year, benchmarks don't stay up to date: Drivers change. New standards emerge. Therefore, we're undergoing a large-scale overhaul of our GPU and CPU tests, documenting exactly how we are benchmarking and what methodologies are being used.Recording benchmark values and taking the time to run the tests in the first place is inherently time-consuming, and Bench is the perfect reason to maintain a source of truth for all enthusiasts. What to read next Here are the tools I use as Tom’s Hardware resident CPU reviewer — a cheap aluminum open bench, thermal paste wipes, platform-labeled external power buttons, and more Hands-on with the Thermal Grizzly Der8enchtable: Updating our longstanding motherboard test bench The GPU benchmarks hierarchy 2026 As for our testing methodologies, here's a glimpse of what's next for our massive database.Revamped data for GPU testing (Image credit: Future)On the GPU side of things, Senior GPU Analyst Jeffrey Kampman has the reins. He's worked out exacting testing methodologies and strategies and outlined them in a separate Tom's Hardware Premium article. For those who are not yet subscribed, we've explained why we're not using frame generation or upscaling technologies like DLSS and FSR, and how we conduct real-world benchmarks. A canned benchmark is handy, but it doesn't represent how it really feels to play a game on the hardware. And more important, it's a more accurate measure of what to expect when playing those titles for yourself.As Jeff explains, he's spent years testing GPUs in both media and dedicated game testing labs, ensuring the integrity of the results on offer. The spread of titles being tested for 2026 is also representative of what players are actually playing, not merely popular benchmarking titles. For example, we've added the single-player epic Expedition 33, in addition to the multiplayer hit Arc Raiders. Adding newer titles to the list, across a range of engines and with Ray Tracing, allows us to build a cohesive outlook on how a given GPU performs in a wide range of scenarios. (Image credit: Nvidia)Updates will be going in phases, first from current-generation hardware like Nvidia's RTX 5000 series and AMD's 9000 series, all the way back to RTX 3000 and the Radeon 6000 lineup. Of course, Intel's offerings are in the mix there too — no matter how small the market share of the dedicated GPU, it'll be being tested for 2026.The three waves of updated tests will take around a month to fully complete, but will offer you a holistic look at the GPU market, and more importantly, offer you granular testing information that you simply can't get anywhere else.A herculean CPU retesting effort (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)CPU Analyst Jake Roach is working alongside Editor-in-Chief and former CPU reviewer Paul Alcorn to deliver a gargantuan update to our CPU tests. Their goal is building a complete dataset of CPU releases that spans over a decade of hardware. For Premium subscribers, you can read Jake's entire methodology behind CPU testing here.Between two and six machines are running concurrently, with two Golden OS images, frozen and duplicated across setups, using the same testing hardware, down to the TIM, to ensure consistent results. Of course, as we are currently testing different platforms, the motherboards used between CPUs differ, but are consistent between rigs.While we are testing hundreds of CPUs, everything from the version of the software to the processes running in the background are identical. For the CPU effort, we'll be going from AMD Excavator and 7th-gen Intel to Zen 5 and Intel Arrow Lake. That's a lot of CPUs, and even more benchmarks. (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)Each modern CPU takes between 10 and 14 hours to test, with some older and slower models taking nearly an entire day, and with the number of chips on offer, it's going to take some time to get things up-to-date. As for the tests we run, things are split into several categories, such as applications, power/efficiency, and gaming workloads. While some tests are automated, each result is manually verified before being uploaded into our database and tools.Application testing goes across a wide range of workloads, from rendering and encoding to code compilation and data science. We're testing power efficiency across benchmarks like Y-Cruncher, Handbrake, and Prime95, just to name a few. Gaming testing is hands-on where possible. The selection of titles is broad and represents real-world players and what people are playing.As a result, no, you're not going to see something like Ashes of the Singularity, which no one plays, but is merely used as a benchmark. Instead, gaming benchmarks are selected across a range of popular titles like Monster Hunter: Wilds, DOOM: The Dark Ages, and Oblivion: Remastered, all of which use wholly different engines, and are being played by many today.Results will begin to trickle out across the database over time, and given the scale of the tests on offer, we will continue to evolve the benchmarking suite, while acknowledging that we might not revisit some of the older architectures on this list again.The benchmarks never stop (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)On the backdrop of a challenging year for consumer hardware, no thanks to the ongoing AI buildout and supply challenges. Getting the most out of what's available is imperative, and Bench gives you access to all of the data you need for whatever workload or game you're looking at within a handful of clicks.We've put a lot of effort into building and maintaining this feature, and we will continue to do so, documenting the process and keeping things nice and fresh for Tom's Hardware Premium subscribers.Maintaining Bench without Tom's Hardware Premium simply isn't possible. It offers us the bandwidth to test, retest, and refine our methodologies outside of a standard CPU cycle or hierarchy update. It also becomes an invaluable reference point for those of you who wish to seek out specific hardware workloads, going beyond our standard reviews and wading through the data yourself.We're never going to stop testing, and Bench is the perfect place to compile our data. As we look forward to the next generation of PC hardware, we'll be sure to keep things up-to-date, giving you access to the cold, hard data behind our testing. In addition to our testing updates, we will also keep working behind the scenes to add new functionality to Bench in the coming months.Tom's Hardware Premium is priced at $3 a month when taking out an annual plan, or $7 for a rolling monthly subscription. Bench is just a single piece of what's on offer. To take advantage of the ultimate resource for enthusiasts, be sure to check it out today.
history.author: The Editors of Tom's Hardware
history.titleZh: 超越评测边界，体验互联网上最详尽的消费者硬件基准数据库——Bench硬件库，涵盖显卡、处理器等数百款产品的全面比较。
history.descriptionZh:
Tom's Hardware 于2025年推出了名为“Tom's Hardware Premium”的付费订阅服务，旨在为科技和DIY计算爱好者提供更深度的内容。该服务的核心亮点之一是名为“Bench”的独家性能分析工具和基准测试数据库。Bench允许用户深入访问各类硬件产品在真实场景和合成基准测试中的详细性能数据，并通过直观的工具进行产品对比和数据分析，为用户选购硬件提供强有力的数据支撑。

**核心功能与操作示例**
Bench的核心功能主要包括两方面：**产品直接对比**和**特定工作负载数据查询**。
1.  **产品对比**：用户可在工具中选择产品类别（如CPU、GPU、笔记本电脑、显示器、SSD），并任意选取两款产品进行详细对比。系统将按字母顺序列出所有已记录的测试数据点。例如，用户可以轻松地将AMD Ryzen 9 9950X3D与Intel Core Ultra 9 285K进行全方位的性能数据对比。
2.  **数据表查询**：用户可按类别浏览完整的性能数据表格。例如，若想了解在特定游戏（如《战神：诸神黄昏》）中、特定分辨率（如1440p）下哪款消费级显卡表现最佳，可直接查询对应的数据表。数据显示，在相近价位段，Radeon 9060 XT在该游戏中的性能领先于NVIDIA RTX 5060，为注重性价比的游戏玩家提供了明确的选购参考。

**数据库的构建、更新与方法论革新**
Bench数据库的数据主要源自Tom's Hardware的硬件评测工作。然而，随着驱动程序更新和新标准的出现，基准测试数据需要持续刷新以保持其参考价值。为此，编辑部正在对GPU和CPU测试进行大规模、系统性的重测与方法论革新。
*   **GPU测试革新**：由高级GPU分析师Jeffrey Kampman主导。新方法论强调**真实游戏体验**，而非仅依赖内置跑分程序。测试将禁用帧生成和DLSS/FSR等超分辨率技术，以反映硬件原生性能。测试游戏阵容基于玩家实际游玩的流行作品（如新增的《远征33》和《Arc Raiders》），涵盖不同引擎和光线追踪场景，以全面评估GPU在各种情境下的表现。更新将分阶段进行，覆盖从当前一代（如NVIDIA RTX 5000系列、AMD 9000系列）回溯至前几代（如RTX 3000系列、Radeon 6000系列）以及Intel的显卡产品，整个过程预计耗时约一个月。
*   **CPU测试革新**：这是一项跨越十余年产品的“海格力斯式”重测工程，由CPU分析师Jake Roach和主编Paul Alcorn共同推进。目标是为从AMD推土机架构、Intel第七代酷睿到最新Zen 5和Arrow Lake架构的数百款CPU建立完整数据集。为确保结果一致性，测试使用克隆的“黄金”操作系统镜像、相同的测试硬件（包括散热硅脂），并严格控制软件版本和后台进程。每颗现代CPU的完整测试需10-14小时，部分老旧型号甚至需要近一天时间。测试类别包括应用程序（渲染、编码、代码编译、数据科学等）、能效（使用Y-Cruncher、Handbrake、Prime95等）和游戏性能。游戏测试同样基于实际流行度选择（如《怪物猎人：荒野》、《毁灭战士：黑暗时代》、《上古卷轴4：湮没 重制版》），摒弃了无人玩但常被用于跑分的游戏。

**技术影响与应用价值**
在当前消费级硬件面临AI热潮冲击和供应链挑战的背景下，帮助用户充分利用现有资源变得至关重要。Bench为用户提供了一个独一无二的“数据源真理”，使其能在几次点击内获取任何特定工作负载或游戏所需的深度性能数据。它超越了标准评测文章的范畴，允许爱好者亲自深入挖掘数据细节。
Tom's Hardware Premium（年费29美元或月费7美元）的订阅模式为维护和持续更新Bench这一庞大工程提供了必要的资源支持，使编辑部能脱离标准的产品发布周期，持续进行测试、重测和方法论优化。未来，编辑部计划继续更新测试数据并为其添加新功能，确保其为PC硬件爱好者提供最新、最全面的性能参考依据。

history.author: Stewart Bendle
history.titleZh: 不足千元，拿下最强游戏CPU、主板及32GB内存——AMD锐龙7 9850X3D处理器、微星MAG X870主板与海盗船复仇者内存组合，新蛋今日套装立省196美元。
history.descriptionZh:
在当前PC硬件价格高企的市场环境下，捆绑销售成为购买高端组件如内存、显卡和固态硬盘时节省开支的有效方式。Newegg近日推出了一款新的组合套餐，延续了其捆绑优惠策略，此次搭配了微星（MSI）一款定价更亲民的主板。

该套餐核心包含三件组件：微星MAG X870 Tomahawk WiFi主板、32GB的 Corsair Vengeance RGB DDR5-6400内存（2x16GB套条），以及AMD锐龙7 9850X3D处理器，捆绑售价为999.99美元。相比1196.98美元的单独零售总价，消费者可节省196.99美元。以低于1000美元的价格获得这三款高性能组件，在目前昂贵的PC硬件市场中显得颇具吸引力。

**技术与规格细节：**
*   **微星MAG X870 Tomahawk WiFi主板**：支持PCIe 5.0高速标准，提供四个M.2插槽，后置I/O面板配备了两个40Gbps的USB-C Lightning端口。该主板原生支持DDR5内存，配备了无需工具的显卡快拆按钮，并提供了高速的WiFi 7无线连接能力。
*   **海盗船复仇者RGB内存**：这套32GB（双通道）DDR5内存运行在6400 MT/s的速度，这是AMD AM5平台处理器的性能甜点频率。由于人工智能热潮推动需求，此类内存价格在过去四五个月内涨幅显著，其在Newegg的单独售价已达437美元。但在此捆绑套餐中，其等效成本仅为241美元，虽仍不低廉，但远低于市面零售价。
*   **AMD锐龙7 9850X3D处理器**：作为AM5平台的新成员，这款8核16线程的处理器凭借略微提升的功耗设定，现已取代9800X3D成为游戏性能最快的CPU。基准测试显示，其性能较9800X3D有所领先，但优势幅度有限。

**套餐价值与应用场景：**
此套餐主要面向旨在构建新高端游戏PC的用户。以不到1000美元的成本，即可获得新电脑三大核心部件（CPU、主板、内存）。用户仅需额外添加固态硬盘（SSD）、显卡（GPU）、机箱、电源（PSU）及散热系统即可完成整机搭建。

**额外赠品：**
购买该套餐还可免费获得两件赠品：价值55.99美元的华硕TUF Gaming M4 Air轻量化游戏鼠标，以及一款标价70美元的RPG游戏《Crimson Desert》。

**总结：**
Newegg此次推出的捆绑套餐，通过将AMD当前顶级的游戏处理器锐龙7 9850X3D、功能全面的微星X870主板及高性能DDR5内存组合销售，在提供显著价格减免（节省约16%）的同时，降低了高端游戏平台的装机门槛。对于计划组建新系统的玩家而言，这是一个极具性价比的核心组件起步方案。

history.title: Nvidia reportedly working on RTX 5050 with 9GB of VRAM on a 96-bit bus, featuring 28 Gbps GDDR7 modules — RTX 5060 with cut-down GB205 GPU also planned
history.description: Nvidia is reportedly working on a new RTX 5050 with 9GB of GDDR7 VRAM, up from 8GB GDDR6 on the existing model. Moving from 20 Gbps chips to 28 Gbps chips, the memory interface is also said to be reduced to 96-bit, from 128-bit on the original. Moreover, an RTX 5060 with a cut-down GB205 die is also said to be in the works, with otherwise same specs.

Subscribe to our newsletter The global component shortage is apparently affecting Nvidia — one of the catalysts of this very crisis — just as hard as other manufacturers, with the consumer segment taking the hit. Memory and chip constraints are leading the company to think of interesting new products — a new RTX 5050 with 9GB of VRAM seems to be in the works, according to leaker @Zed__Wang.New product: RTX5050 9GB GDDR7 96BitNV knows they can give you a 5050/5060 128-bit 12G with the new 3G GDDR7 dies. But no, you got a 5050 9G💀March 4, 2026According to the tweet, this new RTX 5050 will adopt 28 Gbps GDDR7 memory chips in favor of the existing 20 Gbps GDDR6 modules. The VRAM capacity will be upped from 8GB to 9GB, but the bus width will be cut down from 128-bit to just 96-bit. The current spec translates to 320 GB/s of bandwidth, and the updated spec will result in 336 GB/s bandwidth, which is a 5% increase.Go deeper with TH Premium: GPUs (Image credit: Noctua)Desktop RoadmapEnterprise RoadmapRubin in-depthThe Stout Owl: The ultimate Noctua G2 PCThe leaker goes on to mention that Nvidia could build 12GB variants of the RTX 5050 and 5060 with 3GB GDDR7 chips, but perhaps the company isn't concerned with value maximization like that, especially not in these times. The last time a desktop GPU from Nvidia rocked a 96-bit interface was back in 2024 with the RTX 3050, so it's actually not too distant of a memory. The rest of the specs should remain identical; the same GB207 die with 2,560 CUDA cores, built on TSMC's 5nm process, rated at 130W. Clock speed differences (if any) will become public knowledge as we near the potential release of this SKU. The RTX 5050 is one of the few GPUs that basically saw no price hikes in the past few months, only going up about $10. The only other card with the same stability was the RTX 5060. You may like Nvidia RTX 5070 mobile GPU looks set to get more VRAM despite global memory crisis Modders are slapping 32GB of VRAM on the RTX 5080, which isn't good for gamers Micron joins the 3GB GDDR7 party, introduces 36 Gbps modules for GPUs RTX5060 GB205 incomingNV has jammed AIC with 5060Ti 8G, and later realized Oh shit, no GB206 for 5060. And here comes the solution: 5060 based on GB205. Poor AICs need to make a new 5060 PCB design to house the GB205. Basically making a 5070 PCB with an 8-pin connector.March 4, 2026Speaking of which, @Zed__Wang also talked about a new RTX 5060 in the works with a cut-down version of the GB205 GPU — that's the silicon that powers the RTX 5070. Apparently, Nvidia has told AIBs to focus on the 8GB RTX 5060 Ti, which has led to a shortage of GB206 dies for RTX 5060 SKUs. Since both the RTX 5060 and 5060 Ti use the GB206, the chipmaker is now forced to switch gears.Defective GB205 dies might be repurposed to fit into new PCB designs for the RTX 5060 with an 8-pin power connector. Otherwise, the RTX 5070 has a 12V-2x6 connector and the GB205 die inside features 6,144 CUDA cores — those would be reduced to 3,840 CUDA cores for an RTX 5060. Moreover, the bus width on the GB205 is 192-bit and that would also go down, to 128-bit, in order to match an RTX 5060. history.author: Hassam Nasir
history.titleZh: 据报道，英伟达正研发配备9GB显存、96位总线及28 Gbps GDDR7显存的RTX 5050显卡——同时计划推出采用精简版GB205 GPU的RTX 5060型号。
history.descriptionZh:
全球零部件短缺的浪潮正同样冲击着英伟达——这场危机的推手之一。与其他制造商一样，其消费级显卡产品线受到了直接影响。内存与芯片的供应限制正促使该公司思考一些有趣的新产品方案。根据爆料者@Zed__Wang的消息，一款新的RTX 5050显卡似乎正在酝酿中，其最大特点是配备了9GB显存。

据其推文透露，这款新的RTX 5050将采用28 Gbps的GDDR7显存芯片，以取代现有的20 Gbps GDDR6模块。显存容量将从8GB提升至9GB，但显存位宽将从128位大幅削减至96位。当前RTX 5050（假设为8GB 128位 GDDR6）的带宽约为320 GB/s，而新配置（9GB 96位 GDDR7）将带来336 GB/s的带宽，仅提升约5%。

爆料者进一步指出，英伟达本可以使用新的3GB GDDR7芯片制造128位宽、12GB显存的RTX 5050或5060，但公司目前可能并不优先考虑这种价值最大化的方案，尤其是在当前时期。上一次英伟达在桌面GPU上采用96位接口还要追溯到2024年的RTX 3050。预计该卡其余规格将保持不变：基于台积电5nm工艺的GB207芯片，拥有2560个CUDA核心，额定功耗为130W。具体的时钟频率差异（如果有）需待产品发布临近时方能知晓。在过去几个月中，RTX 5050是少数价格基本保持稳定的显卡之一，仅上涨约10美元，另一款是RTX 5060。

此外，@Zed__Wang还透露了一款正在开发中的新RTX 5060，它将采用GB205 GPU的降级版本——而GB205正是RTX 5070所使用的芯片。显然，英伟达已告知AIB合作伙伴重点生产8GB显存的RTX 5060 Ti，这导致了用于RTX 5060的GB206芯片短缺。由于RTX 5060和5060 Ti均使用GB206，英伟达被迫转变策略。

有缺陷的GB205芯片可能会被重新利用，放入为RTX 5060设计的新PCB中，并搭配传统的8针电源接口（而RTX 5070使用的是12V-2x6接口）。GB205芯片原本拥有6144个CUDA核心，为适配RTX 5060，将削减至3840个。同时，其显存位宽也将从192位降至128位，以符合RTX 5060的定位。

**核心技术原理与创新点分析：**
1.  **显存配置的“非典型”调整**：新款RTX 5050的核心创新点在于其“9GB 96位 GDDR7”的混合配置。这并非简单的升级，而是在显存带宽（位宽）、容量（芯片密度）和成本/供应之间进行的权衡。使用更高带宽的GDDR7（28 Gbps）部分抵消了位宽缩减（从128位到96位）带来的带宽损失，最终实现了小幅的净带宽提升（5%）。这种配置在英伟达近年来的产品中较为罕见，是应对特定GDDR7芯片供应状况（例如3GB颗粒的可用性）的直接结果。
2.  **芯片层级的灵活复用策略**：计划中的RTX 5060方案展示了英伟达在芯片层面的灵活应变能力。将原本用于更高端型号（RTX 5070）的GB205芯片，通过屏蔽部分计算单元（CUDA核心）和显存控制器（降低位宽），降级用于主流型号。这不仅解决了GB206芯片短缺的问题，也提高了GB205芯片的总体良率利用率（将缺陷芯片转化为可用产品）。

**性能参数与对比分析：**
*   **RTX 5050 (新款爆料规格) vs. 预期常规升级**：
    *   **爆料规格**：9GB VRAM，96位宽，28 Gbps GDDR7，带宽= 96 * 28 / 8 ≈ **336 GB/s**。
    *   **假设的“常规”128位方案**：若采用128位宽和相同的GDDR7，带宽可达 128 * 28 / 8 = **448 GB/s**。若采用更经济的20 Gbps GDDR6（128位），带宽为 **320 GB/s**。
    *   **对比结论**：新方案的带宽（336 GB/s）仅比旧款GDDR6方案高5%，但远低于理论上的满血GDDR7方案。性能提升将严重依赖于游戏对显存带宽的敏感度，在多数情况下可能感知不强，其核心价值可能仅在于增加了1GB显存容量。
*   **RTX 5060 (GB205降级版) vs. 原版RTX 5060 (GB206)及RTX 5070**：
    *   **计算单元**：降级后CUDA核心数（3840）预计将介于原版RTX 5060（基于GB206，核心数未知但应更少）与RTX 5070（6144）之间，理论上图形计算性能可能强于原版RTX 5060。
    *   **显存子系统**：位宽锁定为128位，这与原版RTX 5060预期一致，但显著低于RTX 5070的192位，这将成为其与RTX 5070性能差距的关键瓶颈之一。
    *   **功耗与设计**：采用GB205芯片但搭配8针供电和简化的PCB，旨在控制成本和功耗，与其主流市场定位相符。

**技术影响与应用场景：**
1.  **对行业的影响**：这反映了在全球供应链持续紧张的背景下，即使是巨头如英伟达也必须采取更务实、有时显得“妥协”的产品策略。通过非常规的规格组合和芯片复用，来保证产品线的持续供应和市场份额。
2.  **对消费者的影响**：
    *   **RTX 5050**：对于预算有限的入门级游戏玩家，新款可能带来在更高纹理质量下略好一点的体验（得益于多出的1GB显存），但整体游戏帧率提升可能有限。其市场吸引力将很大程度上取决于最终定价。
    *   **RTX 5060 (GB205版)**：这款产品可能成为一个有趣的“混合体”。如果定价得当，它可能提供比基于GB206的原版RTX 5060更强的传统光栅化性能，但受限于128位显存位宽，在高分辨率或开启光线追踪等需要高带宽的场景下会遭遇瓶颈。它更适合追求1080p高画质或2K中等画质的游戏玩家。
3.  **应用场景**：这两款调整后的产品主要面向主流PC游戏市场。RTX 5050定位为入门级1080p游戏卡，而RTX 5060则瞄准主流1080p高刷新率和2K入门级游戏。它们的出现，意味着在部件短缺时代，消费者在特定价位段可能需要更仔细地权衡显存容量、带宽和核心性能之间的关系，而非简单地遵循以往的产品层级认知。

总的来说，这些爆料揭示了英伟达在应对供应链挑战时的内部调整逻辑，即优先保证产品上市和线布局的连续性，即使这意味着部分产品会出现非常规的规格配置。最终的市场表现将取决于这些“权衡之作”的定价与实际性能测试结果。

history.titleZh: 七家科技巨头签署特朗普承诺书，避免数据中心用电成本激增
history.descriptionZh:
2026年3月4日，美国总统特朗普召集谷歌、Meta、微软、甲骨文、OpenAI、亚马逊及埃隆·马斯克旗下的xAI等科技企业领导人前往白宫，共同签署了一项名为"费率保护承诺"的协议。该协议要求这些科技公司承诺为其耗能巨大的AI数据中心承担电费开支，以应对两党日益增长的担忧——随着新一代人工智能数据中心的快速建设，美国居民和企业的用电成本可能被推高。

此次会议背景源于美国AI基础设施的爆发式扩张。数据中心作为AI算力的物理载体，其电力需求已对局部电网造成压力，多个社区因担心电价上涨而抵制数据中心的建设。特朗普在签署仪式上指出："科技公司需要公关支持，因为人们认为数据中心建成后电费就会上涨。有些社区因此拒绝数据中心入驻，而现在情况将会逆转。"

协议的核心创新点在于将基础设施发展的成本重新分配。传统上，新建数据中心的电网升级和扩容成本往往通过提高整体电价分摊给所有用户。此次承诺则要求科技企业直接承担相关电费，并可能包括为电网升级提供资金支持。这种模式既保障了社区用户的电价稳定，又为AI基础设施建设扫除了公共阻力。

从行业影响看，这一政策可能加速美国AI数据中心的部署进程。据美国能源信息署数据，2026年数据中心用电量预计将占全国总用电量的8%以上，较2022年增长近三倍。签署协议的七家企业控制着全球约80%的AI算力资源，其承诺将直接影响美国未来五年的电网规划。技术影响方面，该举措可能推动更高效的冷却技术、模块化数据中心设计及可再生能源的直接采购，因为企业为控制电费成本将更积极采用节能方案。

应用场景上，这一承诺主要针对训练大规模AI模型所需的超大规模数据中心。这类设施通常需要50-100兆瓦的持续电力供应，相当于数万户家庭的用电量。协议实施后，科技公司可能会优先在电网容量充足、可再生能源丰富的地区建设数据中心，同时加大与电力公司的长期购电协议谈判。

此次白宫行动反映了美国在AI竞争中的战略调整：通过公私合作模式平衡技术创新与公共利益，既维持企业在全球AI竞赛中的领先地位，又缓解基础设施建设引发的社会矛盾。这种模式若成功实施，可能为其他国家处理类似问题提供政策参考。
